caption: The video shows a close-up of a smartphone screen displaying an application called "Alpha Szenszor eNose v1.0.14." The app is monitoring and acquiring data, as indicated by the text "Acquiring 5/200 (20s)" on the screen. The main display features a graph with multiple lines representing different channels, each labeled with a number and color-coded for easy identification. The graph plots current (in microamperes) against time (in seconds), showing a series of peaks and troughs that suggest some form of periodic measurement or analysis. On the right side of the screen, there are toggle switches for various channels, allowing the user to enable or disable them. A hand is visible in the frame, interacting with the device, possibly adjusting settings or navigating through the app. The overall scene suggests a scientific or technical experiment being conducted using this specialized software.
